item.title: Realizing type-III acoustic Dirac cones with the staggered Mielke model
item.description:
Author(s): Yuan Li, Jingwen Ma, and Xiankai Sun

Dirac cones featuring linearly tilted band diagrams can be classified into type I, II, and III based on their degree of tilting. Type-III Dirac cones combining flat and linear dispersion have been largely unexplored in the acoustic domain. The Mielke model stands out as a potential candidate for sup…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014301] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuan Li, Jingwen Ma, and Xiankai Sun

Dirac cones featuring linearly tilted band diagrams can be classified into type I, II, and III based on their degree of tilting. Type-III Dirac cones combining flat and linear dispersion have been largely unexplored in the acoustic domain. The Mielke model stands out as a potential candidate for sup…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014301] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Realizing type-III acoustic Dirac cones with the staggered Mielke model
item.dc:creator: Yuan Li, Jingwen Ma, and Xiankai Sun

item.dc:subject: Dynamics, dynamical systems, lattice effects

item.title: Domain wall skyrmion-based magnonic crystal
item.description:
Author(s): Zhenyu Wang, Xingen Zheng, Zhixiong Li, Zhizhi Zhang, and Xiansi Wang

A magnonic waveguide based on a domain wall (DW) is considered to be a crucial breakthrough toward the realization of magnonic nanocircuits. However, the effective control of spin waves propagating in DWs remains to be explored. Here, we construct a magnonic crystal (MC) by using a chain of the doma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014402] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhenyu Wang, Xingen Zheng, Zhixiong Li, Zhizhi Zhang, and Xiansi Wang

A magnonic waveguide based on a domain wall (DW) is considered to be a crucial breakthrough toward the realization of magnonic nanocircuits. However, the effective control of spin waves propagating in DWs remains to be explored. Here, we construct a magnonic crystal (MC) by using a chain of the doma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014402] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Domain wall skyrmion-based magnonic crystal
item.dc:creator: Zhenyu Wang, Xingen Zheng, Zhixiong Li, Zhizhi Zhang, and Xiansi Wang

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Magnetic circular dichroism in core-level x-ray photoelectron spectroscopy of altermagnetic ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$ films
item.description:
Author(s): Y. Lytvynenko, A. Akashdeep, T. P. Vo, O. Tkach, S. V. Chernov, A. Gloskovskii, C. Schlueter, C. Luo, V. Ukleev, F. Radu, F. Kronast, T. Hiroto, A. Winkelmann, J. Minár, M. Kläui, G. Schönhense, G. Jakob, H. J. Elmers, and O. Fedchenko

While ferromagnetism and antiferromagnetism are well-established classes of magnetic order, a third class of collinear magnetic order, termed altermagnetism, has recently attracted scientific interest. We measured magnetic circular dichroism (MCD) in core-level photoemission (XPS) at the Ru $2{p}_{3…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014403] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Y. Lytvynenko, A. Akashdeep, T. P. Vo, O. Tkach, S. V. Chernov, A. Gloskovskii, C. Schlueter, C. Luo, V. Ukleev, F. Radu, F. Kronast, T. Hiroto, A. Winkelmann, J. Minár, M. Kläui, G. Schönhense, G. Jakob, H. J. Elmers, and O. Fedchenko

While ferromagnetism and antiferromagnetism are well-established classes of magnetic order, a third class of collinear magnetic order, termed altermagnetism, has recently attracted scientific interest. We measured magnetic circular dichroism (MCD) in core-level photoemission (XPS) at the Ru 2p3/2 an…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014403] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic circular dichroism in core-level x-ray photoelectron spectroscopy of altermagnetic ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$ films

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Angular momentum of vortex-core Majorana zero modes
item.description:
Author(s): Giulia Venditti, Christophe Berthod, and Louk Rademaker

When putting a d+id superconductor on the surface of a three-dimensional topological insulator, the Majorana zero modes (MZMs) appearing inside vortices can have a nontrivial angular momentum. Here, the authors show the emergence of distinct Majorana “flavors”, beyond Chern-based classifications. They also assess the stability and topological protection of these MZMs, as well as possible experimental signatures.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014502] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Giulia Venditti, Christophe Berthod, and Louk Rademaker

When putting a d+id superconductor on the surface of a three-dimensional topological insulator, the Majorana zero modes (MZMs) appearing inside vortices can have a nontrivial angular momentum. Here, the authors show the emergence of distinct Majorana “flavors”, beyond Chern-based classifications. They also assess the stability and topological protection of these MZMs, as well as possible experimental signatures.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014502] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Angular momentum of vortex-core Majorana zero modes
item.dc:creator: Giulia Venditti, Christophe Berthod, and Louk Rademaker

item.title: Revealing superconducting chiral edge modes via resistance distributions
item.description:
Author(s): Linghao Huang, Dongheng Qian, and Jing Wang

Inducing superconducting correlations in quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) states offers a promising route to realize topological superconductivity with chiral Majorana edge modes. However, the definitive identification of these modes is challenging. Here, we propose detecting superconducting chiral edge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014503] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Linghao Huang, Dongheng Qian, and Jing Wang

Inducing superconducting correlations in quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) states offers a promising route to realize topological superconductivity with chiral Majorana edge modes. However, the definitive identification of these modes is challenging. Here, we propose detecting superconducting chiral edge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014503] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026

item.title: Strain-stabilized high-topological-charge skyrmions and field-tunable phase switching in frustrated monolayer ${\mathrm{NiBr}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Zhen Sun, Shiwei Zhu, Xinyuan Guan, Xiaoping Wu, and Changsheng Song

Monolayer ${\mathrm{NiBr}}_{2}$ exhibits strain-tunable topological magnetism mediated by competing exchange interactions on a triangular lattice. Our first-principles calculations show biaxial strain ($ɛ$) controls both the exchange frustration strength $η=|{J}_{1}/{J}_{3}|$ and magnetic anisotropy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024404] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhen Sun, Shiwei Zhu, Xinyuan Guan, Xiaoping Wu, and Changsheng Song

Monolayer NiBr2 exhibits strain-tunable topological magnetism mediated by competing exchange interactions on a triangular lattice. Our first-principles calculations show biaxial strain (ɛ) controls both the exchange frustration strength η=|J1/J3| and magnetic anisotropy energy, with Br-driven spin-o…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024404] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain-stabilized high-topological-charge skyrmions and field-tunable phase switching in frustrated monolayer ${\mathrm{NiBr}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Zhen Sun, Shiwei Zhu, Xinyuan Guan, Xiaoping Wu, and Changsheng Song

item.title: Superconductivity in multi-Weyl semimetals: Conditions for the coexistence of topological and conventional phases
item.description:
Author(s): Alonso Tapia and Enrique Muñoz

In this work, we explore the possible emergence of superconducting phases in a multi-Weyl semimetal. In particular, we show that the presence of a pair of Weyl nodes with chirality $|ν|≥1$ leads to an effective description of the intranodal pairings in terms of monopole harmonics, in contrast to int…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024501] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alonso Tapia and Enrique Muñoz

In this work, we explore the possible emergence of superconducting phases in a multi-Weyl semimetal. In particular, we show that the presence of a pair of Weyl nodes with chirality |ν|≥1 leads to an effective description of the intranodal pairings in terms of monopole harmonics, in contrast to inter…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024501] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconductivity in multi-Weyl semimetals: Conditions for the coexistence of topological and conventional phases

item.title: Gaplessness from disorder and quantum geometry in gapped superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Omri Lesser, Sagnik Banerjee, Xuepeng Wang, Jaewon Kim, Ehud Altman, and Debanjan Chowdhury

It is well known that disorder can induce low-energy Andreev bound states in a sign-changing, but fully gapped, superconductor at $π$ junctions. Generically, these excitations are localized. Starting from a superconductor with a sign-changing and nodeless order parameter in the clean limit, here we …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035109] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Omri Lesser, Sagnik Banerjee, Xuepeng Wang, Jaewon Kim, Ehud Altman, and Debanjan Chowdhury

It is well known that disorder can induce low-energy Andreev bound states in a sign-changing, but fully gapped, superconductor at π junctions. Generically, these excitations are localized. Starting from a superconductor with a sign-changing and nodeless order parameter in the clean limit, here we de…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035109] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Gaplessness from disorder and quantum geometry in gapped superconductors
item.dc:creator: Omri Lesser, Sagnik Banerjee, Xuepeng Wang, Jaewon Kim, Ehud Altman, and Debanjan Chowdhury
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Polarons and bipolarons in the Rydberg-dressed extended Bose-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): G. A. Domínguez-Castro, L. Santos, and L. A. Peña Ardila

The authors explore here how a single and a pair of impurities behave across the superfluid and insulating (charge density wave) phases of a hard-core bosonic bath. In the superfluid regime, an impurity becomes a polaron-like quasiparticle, while in the insulating phase it regains its particle-like nature, moving through a potential landscape shaped by the charge density wave order. Furthermore, the authors show that two impurities can bind into a stable pair even without direct mutual interactions.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035111] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): G. A. Domínguez-Castro, L. Santos, and L. A. Peña Ardila

The authors explore here how a single and a pair of impurities behave across the superfluid and insulating (charge density wave) phases of a hard-core bosonic bath. In the superfluid regime, an impurity becomes a polaron-like quasiparticle, while in the insulating phase it regains its particle-like nature, moving through a potential landscape shaped by the charge density wave order. Furthermore, the authors show that two impurities can bind into a stable pair even without direct mutual interactions.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035111] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Polarons and bipolarons in the Rydberg-dressed extended Bose-Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: G. A. Domínguez-Castro, L. Santos, and L. A. Peña Ardila
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Chiral anomaly-induced nonlinear Hall effect in spin-orbit coupled noncentrosymmetric metals
item.description:
Author(s): Gautham Varma K., Mohd. Hashim Raza, and Azaz Ahmad

Authors of recent studies have shown that the chiral anomaly (CA) is not limited to Weyl semimetals but is also shown by a larger class of materials called spin-orbit-coupled noncentrosymmetric metals (SOC-NCMs), which has shed more insight into the origin of the CA as a Fermi surface property rathe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035112] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gautham Varma K., Mohd. Hashim Raza, and Azaz Ahmad

Authors of recent studies have shown that the chiral anomaly (CA) is not limited to Weyl semimetals but is also shown by a larger class of materials called spin-orbit-coupled noncentrosymmetric metals (SOC-NCMs), which has shed more insight into the origin of the CA as a Fermi surface property rathe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035112] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Chiral anomaly-induced nonlinear Hall effect in spin-orbit coupled noncentrosymmetric metals
item.dc:creator: Gautham Varma K., Mohd. Hashim Raza, and Azaz Ahmad
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Excitons in moiré superlattices with disordered electrons
item.description:
Author(s): Junghwan Kim, Dinh Van Tuan, and Hanan Dery

How do excitons probe electron crystals in moiré superlattices? The authors investigate here how the absorption amplitude and energy of excitons are influenced by charge order at fractional fillings of moiré superlattices. In addition to the sensitivity from s-p orbital mixing of excitonic states that stems from the charge order, this study also demonstrates how the moiré physics is suppressed by atomic defects and thermal fluctuations. This work provides a comprehensive picture that bridges theory and recent experimental observations.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035303] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junghwan Kim, Dinh Van Tuan, and Hanan Dery

How do excitons probe electron crystals in moiré superlattices? The authors investigate here how the absorption amplitude and energy of excitons are influenced by charge order at fractional fillings of moiré superlattices. In addition to the sensitivity from s-p orbital mixing of excitonic states that stems from the charge order, this study also demonstrates how the moiré physics is suppressed by atomic defects and thermal fluctuations. This work provides a comprehensive picture that bridges theory and recent experimental observations.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035303] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Excitons in moiré superlattices with disordered electrons
item.dc:creator: Junghwan Kim, Dinh Van Tuan, and Hanan Dery
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Semiconductors II: surfaces, interfaces, microstructures, and related topics

item.title: Tunable hinge skin states in a hybrid skin-topological sonic crystal
item.description:
Author(s): Yu-Xin Fang, Wen-Hao Zhu, Yu-Ping Lai, Yongyao Li, and Shi-Qiao Wu

Higher-order topological states in sound have played a pivotal role in advancing our understanding of the intricate physics underlying sound transport, leading to new strategies for manipulating sound. In this study, we report a tunable structure for hinge skin states in a non-Hermitian acoustic met…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035407] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Xin Fang, Wen-Hao Zhu, Yu-Ping Lai, Yongyao Li, and Shi-Qiao Wu

Higher-order topological states in sound have played a pivotal role in advancing our understanding of the intricate physics underlying sound transport, leading to new strategies for manipulating sound. In this study, we report a tunable structure for hinge skin states in a non-Hermitian acoustic met…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035407] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunable hinge skin states in a hybrid skin-topological sonic crystal
item.dc:creator: Yu-Xin Fang, Wen-Hao Zhu, Yu-Ping Lai, Yongyao Li, and Shi-Qiao Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Defects potentials for two-dimensional topological materials
item.description:
Author(s): Yuval Abulafia, Amit Goft, Nadav Orion, and Eric Akkermans

Topological materials exhibit distinct electronic characteristics that make them ideal candidates for rapid, reliable, and energy-saving technologies. However, the process of creating these materials is known to be particularly challenging. Here, the authors present a simpler approach to two-dimensional materials by starting with nontopological lattice structures and introducing properly crafted defects to modify their topological properties. These changes are demonstrated through observable electronic characteristics, such as local electron density, offering a clear and practical strategy for designing new topological materials and advancing the creation of more robust quantum and electronic devices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045102] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuval Abulafia, Amit Goft, Nadav Orion, and Eric Akkermans

Topological materials exhibit distinct electronic characteristics that make them ideal candidates for rapid, reliable, and energy-saving technologies. However, the process of creating these materials is known to be particularly challenging. Here, the authors present a simpler approach to two-dimensional materials by starting with nontopological lattice structures and introducing properly crafted defects to modify their topological properties. These changes are demonstrated through observable electronic characteristics, such as local electron density, offering a clear and practical strategy for designing new topological materials and advancing the creation of more robust quantum and electronic devices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045102] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Defects potentials for two-dimensional topological materials
item.dc:creator: Yuval Abulafia, Amit Goft, Nadav Orion, and Eric Akkermans

item.title: Cavity quantum Hall hydrodynamics
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriel Cardoso, Liu Yang, Thors Hans Hansson, and Qing-Dong Jiang

Motivated by recent experiments, we study the coupling of quantum Hall (QH) hydrodynamics to quantum electrodynamics within a resonance cavity. In agreement with experimental observations, we find that the Hall conductivity remains unchanged. However, the coupling to the cavity induces a second-orde…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045108] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriel Cardoso, Liu Yang, Thors Hans Hansson, and Qing-Dong Jiang

Motivated by recent experiments, we study the coupling of quantum Hall (QH) hydrodynamics to quantum electrodynamics within a resonance cavity. In agreement with experimental observations, we find that the Hall conductivity remains unchanged. However, the coupling to the cavity induces a second-orde…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045108] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026

item.title: Quantum Hall effect in a chiral cavity
item.description:
Author(s): Liu Yang, Gabriel Cardoso, Thors Hans Hansson, and Qing-Dong Jiang

We investigate the influence of quantum fluctuations in a chiral cavity on the quantum Hall (QH) state, extending previous studies of QH liquids in linearly polarized cavities. Using the Schrieffer-Wolff transformation for perturbative cavity-matter interaction, we identify the system's normal modes…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045109] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Liu Yang, Gabriel Cardoso, Thors Hans Hansson, and Qing-Dong Jiang

We investigate the influence of quantum fluctuations in a chiral cavity on the quantum Hall (QH) state, extending previous studies of QH liquids in linearly polarized cavities. Using the Schrieffer-Wolff transformation for perturbative cavity-matter interaction, we identify the system's normal modes…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045109] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026

item.title: Interaction-driven Chern insulator at zero electric field in ABCB-stacked tetralayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Yulu Ren, Yang Shen, Chengyang Xu, Wanfei Shan, and Weidong Luo

ABCB-stacked tetralayer graphene, with intrinsic spontaneous polarization, offers a unique platform to explore electron correlation effects, whose interplay with spin-orbit coupling may engender topological phases. Here, employing a $\mathbf{k}·\mathbf{p}$ model with self-consistent Hartree-Fock cal…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045405] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:

ABCB-stacked tetralayer graphene, with intrinsic spontaneous polarization, offers a unique platform to explore electron correlation effects, whose interplay with spin-orbit coupling may engender topological phases. Here, employing a k·p model with self-consistent Hartree-Fock calculations, we invest…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045405] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026

item.title: Sliding ferroelectricity, second harmonic generation, and bulk photovoltaic effect induced by ${60}^{∘}$ twisting in bilayer $\mathrm{PtS}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yiyang Du, Hongsheng Liu, Shi Qiu, Anbing Zhang, Luneng Zhao, Feng Ding, and Junfeng Gao

Intrinsic large, flat saturation surfaces of two-dimensional materials are ideal optical and sliding ferroelectric films. Here, we propose the inversion symmetry of a $\mathrm{PtS}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$ bilayer can be broken in the ${60}^{∘}$ twisted structure with an energy difference of 0.05 eV/($\math…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045409] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yiyang Du, Hongsheng Liu, Shi Qiu, Anbing Zhang, Luneng Zhao, Feng Ding, and Junfeng Gao

Intrinsic large, flat saturation surfaces of two-dimensional materials are ideal optical and sliding ferroelectric films. Here, we propose the inversion symmetry of a PtSe2 bilayer can be broken in the 60∘ twisted structure with an energy difference of 0.05 eV/(PtSe2), compared to the original AA st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045409] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026

item.title: Incommensurate stabilized fractional Chern insulator in alternating twisted trilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Moru Song and Kai Chang

Fractional Chern insulators (FCIs) typically emerge in topological flat bands and are regarded as lattice analogs of fractional quantum Hall states. Conventionally, the flat-band wavefunctions that support FCIs are expected to mimic the lowest Landau level, a condition that can be quantified by the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041101] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026

Fractional Chern insulators (FCIs) typically emerge in topological flat bands and are regarded as lattice analogs of fractional quantum Hall states. Conventionally, the flat-band wavefunctions that support FCIs are expected to mimic the lowest Landau level, a condition that can be quantified by the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041101] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026

item.title: Anomalous thermal broadening in the Shastry-Sutherland model and ${\mathrm{SrCu}}_{2}{({\mathrm{BO}}_{3})}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Zhenjiu Wang, Paul McClarty, Dobromila Dankova, Andreas Honecker, and Alexander Wietek

In the quantum magnet ${\mathrm{SrCu}}_{2}{({\mathrm{BO}}_{3})}_{2}$, an anomalous thermal broadening of the triplon modes has been measured at relatively low temperatures compared to the triplon gap $\mathrm{Δ}$ using both inelastic neutron scattering and Raman spectroscopy. Given how accurately a …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041104] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhenjiu Wang, Paul McClarty, Dobromila Dankova, Andreas Honecker, and Alexander Wietek

In the quantum magnet SrCu2(BO3)2, an anomalous thermal broadening of the triplon modes has been measured at relatively low temperatures compared to the triplon gap Δ using both inelastic neutron scattering and Raman spectroscopy. Given how accurately a broad variety of physical phenomena in SrCu2(B…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041104] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Anomalous thermal broadening in the Shastry-Sutherland model and ${\mathrm{SrCu}}_{2}{({\mathrm{BO}}_{3})}_{2}$

item.title: Pervasive symmetry-lowering nanoscale structural fluctuations in the cuprate ${\mathrm{La}}_{2−x}{\mathrm{Sr}}_{x}\mathrm{Cu}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): R. J. Spieker, M. Spaić, I. Khayr, X. He, D. Zhai, Z. W. Anderson, N. Bielinski, F. Ye, Y. Liu, S. Chi, S. Sarker, M. J. Krogstad, R. Osborn, D. Pelc, and M. Greven

The cuprate superconductors are among the most widely studied quantum materials, yet there remain fundamental open questions regarding their electronic properties and the role of structural degrees of freedom. Recent neutron and x-ray scattering measurements uncovered exponential scaling with temper…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014101] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

Author(s): R. J. Spieker, M. Spaić, I. Khayr, X. He, D. Zhai, Z. W. Anderson, N. Bielinski, F. Ye, Y. Liu, S. Chi, S. Sarker, M. J. Krogstad, R. Osborn, D. Pelc, and M. Greven

The cuprate superconductors are among the most widely studied quantum materials, yet there remain fundamental open questions regarding their electronic properties and the role of structural degrees of freedom. Recent neutron and x-ray scattering measurements uncovered exponential scaling with temper…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014101] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Pervasive symmetry-lowering nanoscale structural fluctuations in the cuprate ${\mathrm{La}}_{2−x}{\mathrm{Sr}}_{x}\mathrm{Cu}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$

item.title: Effective spin model with anisotropic exchange interactions for the spin-orbit coupled Hubbard model at half filling
item.description:
Author(s): Ryo Makuta and Chisa Hotta

Spin-orbit coupling (SOC) in noncentrosymmetric materials is the source of incommensurate magnetic structures. In semiconductors, it drives the Rashba spin splitting and spin momentum locking, while in magnetic insulators based on transition metals, it induces anisotropic spin exchange interactions …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035101] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

Author(s): Ryo Makuta and Chisa Hotta

Spin-orbit coupling (SOC) in noncentrosymmetric materials is the source of incommensurate magnetic structures. In semiconductors, it drives the Rashba spin splitting and spin momentum locking, while in magnetic insulators based on transition metals, it induces anisotropic spin exchange interactions …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035101] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Altermagnetic topological insulators in an anisotropic system
item.description:
Author(s): Xu-Hui Yan, Yi-Xuan Ling, Ying Han, Lu Qi, and Ai-Lei He

Altermagnets, a class of collinear magnetic materials, have become a thriving topic in the fields of condensed matter physics and material science due to their unusual properties and potential applications. Recently, the study of topological states has been extended to the altermagnetic materials. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035102] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:

Altermagnets, a class of collinear magnetic materials, have become a thriving topic in the fields of condensed matter physics and material science due to their unusual properties and potential applications. Recently, the study of topological states has been extended to the altermagnetic materials. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035102] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnetic topological insulators in an anisotropic system
item.dc:creator: Xu-Hui Yan, Yi-Xuan Ling, Ying Han, Lu Qi, and Ai-Lei He
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Thermodynamic paradox and non-Hermitian topological singularities
item.description:
Author(s): Mário G. Silveirinha

Unidirectional lossless closed channels are fundamentally incompatible with thermal equilibrium. Challenging the widespread belief that dissipation alone restores thermodynamic consistency, this work shows that even dissipative nonreciprocal systems can exhibit unphysical thermal sinks and sources. A modal analysis reveals the competing roles of dissipative eigenmodes and their gain-conjugate partners, which together govern fluctuation-induced fields. Thermodynamic consistency is recovered only by accounting for nonlocality, revealing a deep connection between topology, bulk-edge correspondence, and equilibrium in passive systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035103] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mário G. Silveirinha

Unidirectional lossless closed channels are fundamentally incompatible with thermal equilibrium. Challenging the widespread belief that dissipation alone restores thermodynamic consistency, this work shows that even dissipative nonreciprocal systems can exhibit unphysical thermal sinks and sources. A modal analysis reveals the competing roles of dissipative eigenmodes and their gain-conjugate partners, which together govern fluctuation-induced fields. Thermodynamic consistency is recovered only by accounting for nonlocality, revealing a deep connection between topology, bulk-edge correspondence, and equilibrium in passive systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035103] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Thermodynamic paradox and non-Hermitian topological singularities
item.dc:creator: Mário G. Silveirinha

item.title: Terahertz nonlinear response in cuprate superconductors and the Higgs field in doped Mott insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Xiang Li (李翔) and Zheng-Yu Weng

A puzzling phenomenon has been recently revealed in terahertz nonlinear optical response experiments: A third harmonic generation (THG) signal, identified only in the superconducting (SC) phase in a conventional BCS superconductor, is found to persist into a wide pseudogap regime in the underdoped c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035106] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

A puzzling phenomenon has been recently revealed in terahertz nonlinear optical response experiments: A third harmonic generation (THG) signal, identified only in the superconducting (SC) phase in a conventional BCS superconductor, is found to persist into a wide pseudogap regime in the underdoped c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035106] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Higher-order topological systems and their subsymmetry-protected topology
item.description:
Author(s): Myungjun Kang, Wonjun Sung, Sonu Verma, and Sangmo Cheon

Symmetry and topology are essential principles in topological physics. Recently, the idea of subsymmetry-protected topology—where some of the original symmetries are broken while a remaining subset, called subsymmetries, continues to protect specific boundary states—has been developed. Here, we exte…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035107] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Myungjun Kang, Wonjun Sung, Sonu Verma, and Sangmo Cheon

Symmetry and topology are essential principles in topological physics. Recently, the idea of subsymmetry-protected topology—where some of the original symmetries are broken while a remaining subset, called subsymmetries, continues to protect specific boundary states—has been developed. Here, we exte…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035107] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Projected branes as platforms for crystalline, superconducting, and higher-order topological phases
item.description:
Author(s): Archisman Panigrahi and Bitan Roy

Projected branes are constituted by a small subset of sites of a higher-dimensional crystal, otherwise placed on a hyperplane oriented at an irrational or a rational slope therein, for which the effective Hamiltonian is constructed by systematically integrating out the sites of the parent lattice th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035301] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Electron-phonon coupling in Kekulé-ordered graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Dominik Szczęśniak

Owing to its unique characteristics, graphene is inherently susceptible to instabilities that can give rise to novel ordered phases. One representative example is the bond-density wave, which leads to Kekulé-distorted structure with alternating bond lengths, accompanied by modified electronic and ph…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035402] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

Owing to its unique characteristics, graphene is inherently susceptible to instabilities that can give rise to novel ordered phases. One representative example is the bond-density wave, which leads to Kekulé-distorted structure with alternating bond lengths, accompanied by modified electronic and ph…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035402] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Generalized strain-induced pseudomagnetic fields in hexagonal Dirac materials
item.description:
Author(s): Jiawei Xia and Shuze Zhu

Strain engineering is effective for tuning the electronic properties of Dirac materials via pseudomagnetic fields. However, current understanding of pseudo-Landau levels and pseudospin polarization is largely limited to graphene, leaving other complex hexagonal lattices with Dirac cones uncharted fo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035403] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

However, current understanding of pseudo-Landau levels and pseudospin polarization is largely limited to graphene, leaving other complex hexagonal lattices with Dirac cones uncharted fo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035403] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Ground state phase diagram and Haldane-like symmetry-protected phase in the $S=\frac{1}{2}$ Heisenberg model on the two-dimensional square-hexagon-octagon lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Yumeng Luo, Yuehong Li, Mengfan Jiang, Muwei Wu, Jian-Jian Yang, Dao-Xin Yao, and Han-Qing Wu

Using stochastic series expansion quantum Monte Carlo and density matrix renormalization group methods, we investigate the ground-state phase diagram of the $S=1/2$ Heisenberg model on the two-dimensional square-hexagon-octagon (SHO) lattice. The model incorporates nearest-neighbor interactions ${J}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045103] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

Using stochastic series expansion quantum Monte Carlo and density matrix renormalization group methods, we investigate the ground-state phase diagram of the S=1/2 Heisenberg model on the two-dimensional square-hexagon-octagon (SHO) lattice. The model incorporates nearest-neighbor interactions J1 (in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045103] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

Rev. B 90, 205403 (2014)…

item.title: Tunneling spectroscopy of the spinon-Kondo effect in one-dimensional Mott insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Rodrigo G. Pereira, Bruno F. Marquez, Karen Hallberg, Tim Bauer, and Reinhold Egger

We study the tunneling density of states (TDOS) in one-dimensional (1D) Mott insulators at energies below the charge gap. By employing nonlinear Luttinger liquid theory and density-matrix renormalization group (DMRG) simulations, we predict that in the presence of a magnetic impurity at the boundary…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045402] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:

We study the tunneling density of states (TDOS) in one-dimensional (1D) Mott insulators at energies below the charge gap. By employing nonlinear Luttinger liquid theory and density-matrix renormalization group (DMRG) simulations, we predict that in the presence of a magnetic impurity at the boundary…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045402] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Non-Hermitian Su-Schrieffer-Heeger model with the energy levels of free parafermions
item.description:
Author(s): Edward McCann

Using a parent Hermitian tight-binding model on a bipartite lattice with chiral symmetry, we theoretically generate non-Hermitian models for free fermions with $p$ orbitals per unit cell satisfying a complex generalization of chiral symmetry. The $p$ complex energy bands in $k$ space are given by a …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045403] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Edward McCann

Using a parent Hermitian tight-binding model on a bipartite lattice with chiral symmetry, we theoretically generate non-Hermitian models for free fermions with p orbitals per unit cell satisfying a complex generalization of chiral symmetry. The p complex energy bands in k space are given by a common…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045403] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Breakdown of the symmetry constraint in a Floquet topological insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Ming-Jian Gao and Jun-Hong An

A topological insulator is regarded as an ideal candidate for information storage and high-speed lossless electrical transmission devices due to robust topological protected boundary modes. Previous studies revealed that symmetry exerts an unbreakable constraint on the existence, classes, and orders…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041103] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

A topological insulator is regarded as an ideal candidate for information storage and high-speed lossless electrical transmission devices due to robust topological protected boundary modes. Previous studies revealed that symmetry exerts an unbreakable constraint on the existence, classes, and orders…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041103] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026

item.title: Fact and artifact in Lorentz imaging of skyrmionic magnetic textures
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaowen Chen, Luyan Yang, Andrii Savchenko, Donghai Yang, Wen Shi, Thibaud Denneulin, Nikolai S. Kiselev, Lei Jin, Dongsheng Song, Rafal E. Dunin-Borkowski, and Fengshan Zheng

Lorentz transmission electron microscopy is widely used for imaging magnetic structures at the nanoscale in real space. However, its interpretation can be complicated by imaging artifacts induced by the defocus distance and specimen tilt. In this work, we systematically examine the origins of such a…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214455] Published Tue Dec 30, 2025
item.content:encoded:

Lorentz transmission electron microscopy is widely used for imaging magnetic structures at the nanoscale in real space. However, its interpretation can be complicated by imaging artifacts induced by the defocus distance and specimen tilt. In this work, we systematically examine the origins of such a…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214455] Published Tue Dec 30, 2025

item.title: Interplay of Kondo coupling and geometrical frustration in weakly coupled ruthenate pyrochlore ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{1.1}{\mathrm{Nd}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Ru}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$
item.description:
Author(s): Sairam Ithineni, Soumyakanta Panda, Hanuma Kumar Dara, Ratiranjan Samal, Ajaya K. Nayak, Niharika Mohapatra, Diptikanta Swain, and D. Samal

The systems exhibiting coexistence of Kondo coupling and magnetic frustration offer a rich platform for exploring the interplay between these competing effects, which often give rise to exotic quantum phases. Rare-earth-doped metallic pyrochlores with noncoplanar spin configurations, pronounced Isin…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214457] Published Tue Dec 30, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sairam Ithineni, Soumyakanta Panda, Hanuma Kumar Dara, Ratiranjan Samal, Ajaya K. Nayak, Niharika Mohapatra, Diptikanta Swain, and D. Samal

The systems exhibiting coexistence of Kondo coupling and magnetic frustration offer a rich platform for exploring the interplay between these competing effects, which often give rise to exotic quantum phases. Rare-earth-doped metallic pyrochlores with noncoplanar spin configurations, pronounced Isin…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214457] Published Tue Dec 30, 2025

item.title: Role of transition metal based kagome lattice in the physical properties of ${\mathrm{LaIr}}_{5}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaRh}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$, and ${\mathrm{YRu}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): H. Y. Uzunok, S. Baǧcı, İ. Sümer, K. Başhan, and H. M. Tütüncü

Recent research on kagome compounds has pointed out their multiple applications in diverse fields. Here, we have reported the physical properties of hexagonal superconductors ${\mathrm{LaIr}}_{5}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaRh}}_{3}{\mathrm{…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214520] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): H. Y. Uzunok, S. Baǧcı, İ. Sümer, K. Başhan, and H. M. Tütüncü

Recent research on kagome compounds has pointed out their multiple applications in diverse fields. Here, we have reported the physical properties of hexagonal superconductors LaIr5, LaIr3B2, LaIr3Ga2, LaRh3B2, and YRu3B2, in which transition metal atoms form a kagome lattice. In our ab initio calcul…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214520] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Role of transition metal based kagome lattice in the physical properties of ${\mathrm{LaIr}}_{5}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaRh}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$, and ${\mathrm{YRu}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ superconductors

item.title: Noncollinear magnetic multipoles in collinear altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Luca Buiarelli, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Turan Birol

Altermagnets host an array of magnetic multipoles, which are often visualized and studied in reciprocal space. In real space, the relative phase of the multipoles of the spin density around atoms determines whether a system is an altermagnet or a conventional antiferromagnet. In this study, we appro…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224442] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luca Buiarelli, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Turan Birol

Altermagnets host an array of magnetic multipoles, which are often visualized and studied in reciprocal space. In real space, the relative phase of the multipoles of the spin density around atoms determines whether a system is an altermagnet or a conventional antiferromagnet. In this study, we appro…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224442] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025

item.title: Electronic structure and phonon properties in the kagome metals $R{T}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ ($R=\mathrm{Y},\mathrm{Lu}$; $T=\mathrm{Co},\mathrm{Os}$)
item.description:
Author(s): Savita Chaudhary, Armando Consiglio, Jaskaran Singh, Domenico Di Sante, Ronny Thomale, and Yogesh Singh

The $R{T}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ ($R=\mathrm{Y},\mathrm{Lu}, T=\mathrm{Co},\mathrm{Os}$) family hosts a perfect kagome lattice of $T$ atoms, offering an interesting platform to investigate the interplay of electronic structure, superconductivity, and lattice dynamics. Here, we compare two members of t…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235170] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Savita Chaudhary, Armando Consiglio, Jaskaran Singh, Domenico Di Sante, Ronny Thomale, and Yogesh Singh

The RT3B2 (R=Y,Lu, T=Co,Os) family hosts a perfect kagome lattice of T atoms, offering an interesting platform to investigate the interplay of electronic structure, superconductivity, and lattice dynamics. Here, we compare two members of this family, LuOs3B2 and YCo3B2, with similar crystallography …


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235170] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Electronic structure and phonon properties in the kagome metals $R{T}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ ($R=\mathrm{Y},\mathrm{Lu}$; $T=\mathrm{Co},\mathrm{Os}$)
item.dc:creator: Savita Chaudhary, Armando Consiglio, Jaskaran Singh, Domenico Di Sante, Ronny Thomale, and Yogesh Singh

item.title: Topological flat-band engineering in frustrated graphene antidot lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoyi Yuan, Jiawei Xia, and Shuze Zhu

Realizing materials with intrinsic flat bands holds significant potential for accessing correlated and topological phases. In this work, via large-scale tight-binding calculations, we demonstrate that pairing frustrated quantum antidots in a hexagonal lattice yields intrinsically tunable flat bands.…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235435] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoyi Yuan, Jiawei Xia, and Shuze Zhu

Realizing materials with intrinsic flat bands holds significant potential for accessing correlated and topological phases. In this work, via large-scale tight-binding calculations, we demonstrate that pairing frustrated quantum antidots in a hexagonal lattice yields intrinsically tunable flat bands.…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235435] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Topological flat-band engineering in frustrated graphene antidot lattices

item.title: Probing unoccupied Yb $5d$ states in ${\mathrm{YbAl}}_{3}$ by ${L}_{\text{III}}$-edge x-ray absorption spectroscopy
item.description:
Author(s): Norimasa Sasabe, Masafumi Gotoda, Kojiro Mimura, Takayuki Uozumi, Hitoshi Sato, Naomi Kawamura, Shigeo Ohara, and Masaichiro Mizumaki

Yb $3d$ x-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) and $L$-edge x-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS) are powerful techniques for determining the valence state of Yb compounds. In particular, $L$-edge XAS directly probes optical transitions from the Yb $2p$ core level into unoccupied Yb $5d$ states, there…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245164] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Norimasa Sasabe, Masafumi Gotoda, Kojiro Mimura, Takayuki Uozumi, Hitoshi Sato, Naomi Kawamura, Shigeo Ohara, and Masaichiro Mizumaki

Yb 3d x-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) and L-edge x-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS) are powerful techniques for determining the valence state of Yb compounds. In particular, L-edge XAS directly probes optical transitions from the Yb 2p core level into unoccupied Yb 5d states, thereby providi…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245164] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Probing unoccupied Yb $5d$ states in ${\mathrm{YbAl}}_{3}$ by ${L}_{\text{III}}$-edge x-ray absorption spectroscopy
item.dc:creator: Norimasa Sasabe, Masafumi Gotoda, Kojiro Mimura, Takayuki Uozumi, Hitoshi Sato, Naomi Kawamura, Shigeo Ohara, and Masaichiro Mizumaki
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Spin stripes in the Hubbard model: A combined dynamical mean-field theory and Bethe-Salpeter analysis
item.description:
Author(s): Ruslan Mushkaev, Francesco Petocchi, Shintaro Hoshino, and Philipp Werner

The Hubbard model is known to accommodate various electronic orders, including stripes, which are important for understanding the physics of cuprates. We study spin-stripe order in the square lattice Hubbard model as a function of doping and temperature, by solving the Bethe-Salpeter equation with t…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245165] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ruslan Mushkaev, Francesco Petocchi, Shintaro Hoshino, and Philipp Werner

The Hubbard model is known to accommodate various electronic orders, including stripes, which are important for understanding the physics of cuprates. We study spin-stripe order in the square lattice Hubbard model as a function of doping and temperature, by solving the Bethe-Salpeter equation with t…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245165] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Spin stripes in the Hubbard model: A combined dynamical mean-field theory and Bethe-Salpeter analysis
item.dc:creator: Ruslan Mushkaev, Francesco Petocchi, Shintaro Hoshino, and Philipp Werner
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Charge pumps, boundary modes, and the necessity of unnecessary criticality
item.description:
Author(s): Abhishodh Prakash and S. A. Parameswaran

We link the presence of “unnecessary” quantum critical surfaces within a single gapped phase of matter to the nontrivial topology of families of gapped Hamiltonians that encircle the critical surface. We study a specific set of one-dimensional spin models where each such family forms a one-parameter…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, L241117] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Abhishodh Prakash and S. A. Parameswaran

We link the presence of “unnecessary” quantum critical surfaces within a single gapped phase of matter to the nontrivial topology of families of gapped Hamiltonians that encircle the critical surface. We study a specific set of one-dimensional spin models where each such family forms a one-parameter…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, L241117] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Charge pumps, boundary modes, and the necessity of unnecessary criticality
item.dc:creator: Abhishodh Prakash and S. A. Parameswaran

item.title: Noncentrosymmetric Mott ground state in the breathing kagome lattice ${\mathrm{Nb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cl}}_{8}$
item.description:
Author(s): Keyi Li, Xin Han, Hao Li, Helin Mei, Mingshu Tan, Bingru Lu, Xiang Li, Wenbo Sang, Xinbo Wang, Anmin Zhang, Jianting Ji, Youguo Shi, Feng Jin, and Qingming Zhang

The breathing Kagome material ${\mathrm{Nb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cl}}_{8}$ has recently been identified as a single-band Mott insulator and theoretically predicted to host coexisting ferroelectricity, magnetism, and ferrovalley—yet experimental verification of these predictions remains lacking. Here, throug…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214116] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Keyi Li, Xin Han, Hao Li, Helin Mei, Mingshu Tan, Bingru Lu, Xiang Li, Wenbo Sang, Xinbo Wang, Anmin Zhang, Jianting Ji, Youguo Shi, Feng Jin, and Qingming Zhang

The breathing Kagome material Nb3Cl8 has recently been identified as a single-band Mott insulator and theoretically predicted to host coexisting ferroelectricity, magnetism, and ferrovalley—yet experimental verification of these predictions remains lacking. Here, through polarization- and temperatur…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214116] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.dc:title: Noncentrosymmetric Mott ground state in the breathing kagome lattice ${\mathrm{Nb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cl}}_{8}$
item.dc:creator: Keyi Li, Xin Han, Hao Li, Helin Mei, Mingshu Tan, Bingru Lu, Xiang Li, Wenbo Sang, Xinbo Wang, Anmin Zhang, Jianting Ji, Youguo Shi, Feng Jin, and Qingming Zhang

item.prism:section: Structure, structural phase transitions, mechanical properties, defects

item.title: Quantized quadrupole superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Yun-Mei Li, Yongwei Huang, and Kai Chang

We introduce a class of superconductors termed “quantized quadrupole superconductors” that support Majorana corner modes according to the bulk-corner correspondence, distinct from previous works on the second-order topological superconductors. An intrinsic physical quantity for superconductors, i.e.…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214519] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yun-Mei Li, Yongwei Huang, and Kai Chang

We introduce a class of superconductors termed “quantized quadrupole superconductors” that support Majorana corner modes according to the bulk-corner correspondence, distinct from previous works on the second-order topological superconductors. An intrinsic physical quantity for superconductors, i.e.…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214519] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantized quadrupole superconductors
item.dc:creator: Yun-Mei Li, Yongwei Huang, and Kai Chang
